I concur with the selection of option B as the accurate representation of Hutchinson's triad. My agreement stems primarily from the consistency in current diagnostic criteria which prioritize sensorineural deafness over other forms like conductive deafness due to its intrinsic involvement in the neural pathways crucial for sound processing. Additionally, while malformed molars do appear across various congenital conditions, they have been specifically noted alongside interstitial keratitis and sensorineural deafness in descriptions of Hutchinson-Gilford Progeria Syndrome, supporting the accuracy of including all three symptoms together under this diagnosis.
